structuredPlan.goal: Audit HELiXiR's current scoring system and propose a new Lit-specific health dimension with detection methods, metrics, and weight recommendations to enable framework-aware component analysis.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Documented audit of all existing analyzers identifying Lit-specific gaps and currently unmeasured patterns
Proposed 'Lit Patterns' dimension with at least 5 sub-metrics across reactive properties, lifecycle, render efficiency, shadow DOM, and event patterns
Detection method specifications for each metric defining whether detection is CEM-based, source-based, or hybrid
Tier (Critical/Important/Advanced) and weight recommendation for new dimension with impact justification
Impact analysis demonstrating grade changes on 3+ real HELiX components when dimension is applied

tasks.title: Audit existing analyzers and dimension system
tasks.description: Review all analyzer implementations in src/analyzers/ to understand current scoring architecture, study the dimension registry to understand weight and tier configuration, and document Lit-specific gaps across all currently measured dimensions.

tasks.title: Test baseline HELiXiR scoring output
tasks.description: Execute HELiXiR MCP score_component and score_all_components tools on 3-5 HELiX library components to observe baseline scoring, current dimension weights, and output format to understand what is already measured.

tasks.title: Analyze CEM-Source Fidelity analyzer pattern
tasks.description: Study the CEM-Source Fidelity analyzer implementation to understand how it combines CEM metadata with source code analysis, as this pattern will be the template for Lit-specific detection methods.

tasks.title: Research and document Lit best practices patterns
tasks.description: Create comprehensive research document covering: (1) Reactive Property Usage with @property/@state distinctions, type declarations, reflect/attribute options, and custom converters; (2) Lifecycle patterns including connectedCallback cleanup, willUpdate versus updated usage, and requestUpdate anti-patterns; (3) Render efficiency covering template directives, @query usage, and static styles; (4) Shadow DOM patterns with CSS custom properties, ::part() exports, slot composition, and adoptedStyleSheets; (5) Event patterns including custom event typing, bubbling/composed configuration, and listener cleanup.

tasks.title: Design Lit Patterns dimension specification
tasks.description: Create formal dimension specification including dimension metadata, at least 5 scorable sub-metrics with clear scoring logic and thresholds, detection approach for each metric, proposed weight distribution, tier assignment (Critical/Important/Advanced), and justification for integration into the scoring system.

tasks.title: Document detection methods for each pattern
tasks.description: Specify precise detection approach for each Lit pattern sub-metric: what signals to detect, where to find them (CEM metadata fields or source AST nodes), implementation approach, confidence level, false positive risks, and working code examples for each detection method.

tasks.title: Conduct dimension impact analysis
tasks.description: Score 3-5 HELiX components under both current system and with proposed Lit Patterns dimension applied, document all resulting grade changes, verify no weight conflicts with existing dimensions, and provide quantitative evidence for dimension weight calibration.

tasks.title: Compile expert audit report
tasks.description: Synthesize all audit findings, dimension proposal, detection specifications, and impact analysis into comprehensive expert audit report with clear recommendations on whether/how to implement Lit Patterns dimension, priority ranking of sub-metrics, and concrete next implementation steps.

deviationRules.condition: If a Lit pattern cannot be reliably detected from CEM metadata alone
deviationRules.action: Specify AST-based source analysis detection method using existing source-analysis library pattern; if source analysis also proves infeasible, mark pattern as aspirational for future runtime analysis phase

deviationRules.condition: If proposed dimension weight creates grade redistribution skew or conflicts with existing dimensions
deviationRules.action: Iteratively adjust sub-metric weights and dimension weight, prioritize critical/security patterns over advanced patterns, and document all weight calibration rationale in report

deviationRules.condition: If certain Lit patterns require runtime analysis or behavioral testing to detect
deviationRules.action: Mark as out-of-scope for current static analysis phase, document prerequisites, and propose for future enhancement with runtime instrumentation

deviationRules.condition: If impact analysis reveals significant unintended grade redistribution across sample components
deviationRules.action: Validate dimension design with expert review, consider phased rollout starting with preview mode, and document redistribution justification in report
